sections_cited: Section 17-A of the Family Court Act

chunks.text: Order Sheet IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH KARACHI CP No.S-157 of 2024 ____________________________________________________________________ Date Order with signature(s) of Judge(s) 1. For orders on CMA No.3787/2025 2. For orders on CMA No.9979/2024 3. For orders on CMA No.1756/2024 4. For hearing of main case 17.11.2025 Petitioner, Muhammad Kazim, present in person Mr. Naveed advocate for the respondent --------- This petition has been directed against the order dated 14.11.2023 passed by learned IXth Civil & Family Judge Malir at Karachi, whereby an application under Section 17-A of the Family Court Act, 1964, for grant of interim maintenance was allowed. Learned trial Court had directed that the petitioner shall pay an amount of Rs.15000/- to each minor per month. It transpires from record that vide order dated 06.02.2024, interim maintenance of each minor was reduced to Rs.10,000/- per month, which per petitioner, he was regularly paying. Learned counsel for the respondent submits that the matter has been proceeded before trial Court and it is fixed for announcement of judgment before the learned trial Court on 22.11.2025. This petition is disposed of with the observation that interim maintenance fixed for minors shall be treated as Rs.10,000/- and will be subject to the final outcome of the Suit and if learned trial Court is of the view that said interim maintenance is not sufficient, it may enhance the maintenance after appraisal of the evidence of the parties. The learned trial Court is directed to decide the case expeditiously and it is expected that on 22.11.2025 when the matter is fixed for announcement of judgment, learned trial Court shall decide it in accordance with the law.

This petition is disposed of with the observation that interim maintenance fixed for minors shall be treated as Rs.10,000/- and will be subject to the final outcome of the Suit and if learned trial Court is of the view that said interim maintenance is not sufficient, it may enhance the maintenance after appraisal of the evidence of the parties. The learned trial Court is directed to decide the case expeditiously and it is expected that on 22.11.2025 when the matter is fixed for announcement of judgment, learned trial Court shall decide it in accordance with the law. Office is directed to send a copy of this order to the learned trial Court through learned District & Sessions Judge, Malir at Karachi, today, for information. JUDGE Nadir*
